methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Quantum Computing. Range: $500M - $5.5B.

data.narrative:
Infleqtion is a Series C quantum computing company seeking valuation guidance. The target raised $210M to date against a last post-money valuation of $1.8B. The relevant comp universe spans the quantum computing sector and includes three public comparables (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) valued between $500M and $5.5B, alongside eight private peers including Quantinuum and Q-CTRL.

Two valuation methods triangulate materially different implications. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $3.1B. Peer-median methodology anchors to the median valuation of three same-sector comparables at $2.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.5B. The disparity between methods reflects uncertainty inherent in a nascent, heterogeneous sector where public and private valuations diverge substantially.

Synthesis across methods supports a valuation range of $1.7B to $3.5B, with a median point of $2.5B. The $1.7B floor reflects the current post-money valuation; the $3.5B ceiling derives from stage-progression logic. The peer-median estimate of $2.0B anchors conservatively within this range. Key uncertainties include the predictive validity of the 1.7x multiplier in quantum computing—a sector with volatile public comparables—and the heterogeneity of business models and technology approaches among peers, limiting direct comparability.
